reviews.review_msg: Ian is very nice! his tests are fair! just that he moved too fast when he explained slides. Overall, GOOD!!

Well, he is easy and nice to every student. BUT THE TAs ARE stupid, coz Scott said the answer close to 0.47 (0.40-55)are both right for the answer for the midterm, BUT the TA need you to get exactly answer. I really want to know how to TA can be TA ! The marking is not fair coz of the TA. Please Scott, tell the TA not to mark so hard on the exam

As others have mentioned, her TAs are quite difficult graders for a first year course. I was taking third year sociology courses at the same time and receiving much better marks. It was a bit of a deterrent for me for engaging with more GSWS courses, as what is expected overall is just a bit unclear.

Carmen is a great prof. However, the issues lay within the TA's. My TA marked very subjectively, and would deduct marks for things not asked for in the questions. If your work didn't align with their opinions, marks were deducted as well it seems. Rubric was released after assignments were handed back, which made it hard to know what was wanted.

I did 100 with them, criteria was clear, assignments were fair, exams were fair. Now in 101, rubric is incredibly subjective, only revealed rubric after the exam had been graded. Exam material focuses on one specific module. Strict criteria for one 100 word question, yet only revealed this after the fact. Prof is passionate, do 100 as a gpa booster
They're nice and passionate about gender studies. Also makes cool assignments.

The prof is kind, approachable, and responsive to all emails in a timely manner. Only major suggestion is to implement a clear grading rubric for essays. Also, her and the TAs deduct marks for not including a minor point from readings in essays, even if that point was stated in one sentence and was not the main thesis. Very very subjective grading
Is like an english teacher in highschool, if it is not the one answer that they expect then good luck at getting a good grade. No wiggle room for understanding concepts or even your own thoughts.

Took this course last semester. The professor is very nice, clear in his slides although his talking wonders around sometimes. Make sure you have a good relationship with the TA. Relate the assignments to your personal life and show your interest in the assignments. You will be guaranteed a good score! Got A+ :)

Took this course last semester. The professor is very nice, clear in his slides although his talking wonders around sometimes. Make sure you have a good relationship with the TA. Relate the assignments to your personal life and show your interest in the assignments. You will be guaranteed a good score! Got A+ :)

Took this course last semester. The professor is very nice, clear in his slides although his talking wonders around sometimes. Make sure you have a good relationship with the TA. Relate the assignments to your personal life and show your interest in the assignments. You will be guaranteed a good score! Got A+ :)

definitely one of the most flakey profs ever. She doesn't let you know if she is going to ghost the WHOLE class she left for MONTHS without notice and didn't respond to emails. No idea of my standing in the class up until roughly last week. Super unprofessional and SFU is always promoting learning Indigneous knowledge.. hypocrisy..

Save yourself some sanity and don't take INDG 101 with this professor. Wait until you can get a better professor because this prof literally does NOT reply emails or grade assignments.

Sent an email over a month ago and she didn't reply. NONE of our assignments have been marked and it's already the end of the term. 

AND WHERE ARE THE TA'S???

Learning material was interesting, but the way in which the professor made lessons extremely monotonous. Prof would take 3 hours to present 20 slides, relying on 1h+ videos to teach. Consistent spelling & grammatical errors in her slides and messages. Also, inconsistencies between most verbal instructions, criteria and guidelines given in messages.

Wasn't hard but the prof made it more challenging than it needed to be. Her instructions were SO unclear and she had spelling errors and mistakes throughout her slides and tests. She cancelled so many lectures so the class was very behind. We literally did the last unit of our class on the same day as the test and we had to take it right after.
She does not answer emails, She has several spelling errors throughout all her slides and canvas messages, and she contradicts her assignment instructions.
No Comments

Punja is one of the best profs I've had at SFU. His lectures are very clear and engaging, his exams are straight from his lecture notes. DO NOT MEMORIZE TEXTBOOK ONLY HIS NOTES WORD FOR WORD. If you do that then you will get good mark. Would definetly take a class again if he was teaching it, recommend 100%.

He is such an amazing prof if u need to take 101 just take it with him you will end up understanding everything and also getting a good grade. His lectures are crystal clear and you would love to go to his lectures lol which u kinda have to :)
nice prof, hard course

NOPE! not an open book exam, not a GPA booster. The professor is good he's pretty humble and a nice professor, but I couldn't follow or comprehend what he was saying most of the time. The exams are some what easy, but VERY "wordy" For the sake of your mentality, don't take it. BUT, if you're willing to make an effort ON YOUR OWN, you do you.

Very boring and disorganized lectures. Cancelled 3 of the lectures due to being sick. Was not active with checking email nor canvas. Was not helpful at all. Would never recommend this prof to anyone. Worst experience ive had at SFU.
Great class

One of the better lectures at SFU by far. you can get an A as long you do the work. Kinney makes his lectures interesting as well as relevant. Exams are fair, and if you have a serious legit issue- he'll take it into account. Does it get any better?
What is this guy doing here? Such a useless professor.  Thinks that crime can be understood using statistics.  Painful.
boring, dull, never gets to the point!
Moves around to much in class. Works very hard for his students
cute as tellytubby and about as insightful... To begin with quite liked the cheerleader stuff... doesn't really make you think, gives you ready  made answers... all rather simple
Only reason I'm in his tutorial is because he is semi good looking.  Wonder if he has any food in his beard.
Very passionate teacher, but very unfriendly and unapproachable in my opinion. Very tough marker for a first year class.

Her informal teaching style is both good and bad. Exams are pretty fair for the most part. They're "multiple guess" which makes them pretty straight forward. She bring in 'real life' examples when teaching the course mt which is very helpful

It is an Intro class so it can be boring. You cover a lot of material; seemed like a new topic every day. Yercich kept it interesting and entertaining though. Good lectures and in class assignments. She is nice and approachable. Very helpful in class and office hours.
She was ok. The lectures were kinda dry.

Reasonable and helpful but sorta tough grader. Dont slack cuz you wont do well. If you put the effort in and ask for help when you need it youll do fine. 2 exams. 1 proposal. 1 paper. Participation. Clear about whats required. Not hard but make sure you prepare.
Made 3 hour night classes entertaining. Good lectures w examples and exercises that made sense. Great w what we needed. Gave extensions, bonus marks, etc. Easy to talk to and responded w in a day.

Bryan is a hilarious proff in both announcements and lecs, if you had serious lecs all day his sarcastic humour is needed. Yeah, he goes off-topic a bit but most of the time they are actually extensive examples, you just gotta pay attention. PLIS interact/ask questions in lec, there are only 2/3 ppl normally that appear to be even looking up.

This class is a very easy grade if you are willing to put in a little effort. However, the lectures are painfully boring and Kinney is not engaging. Midterm and final are online, but insanely long and textbook specific, even though textbook is incorrect and outdated. Also, my TA did not show up for a single tutorial.

he is such a nice guy. his lectures can sometimes get off topic but he is funny and tells a lot of anecdotes. if you read the textbook or even just have it, the exams are easy (they are open book). weekly participation can be annoying but is overall easy marks.
amazing class amazing professor. read the textbook and you'll be fine for the midterm and final. do the packsacks every week for easy grades. 10/10 class I loved it and the professor is great!

This professor is a very sweet person who understands his students needs. He is considerate of us and it is not going unnoticed. Pretty easy course material, just make sure to read the textbook. Amazing guy 10/10 would take it again

He is a super easy professor, but his lectures are hard to listen to; he seems to go off-topic a lot. Participation in tutorials is important. Exams are easy and open-book, and you get a week to do them!

he was a super nice guy but showed up for like half the classes, You should definitely take 101 with him even though he doesn't show up and has bad lectures because it's so easy. Easily the worst lectures I've ever had and you can and should just read the textbooks instead.

I felt bad for the guy because he seemed really sweet but unless you want to hear about his cat or how he failed at being a fighter pilot I wouldn't recommend lectures. Exams are literally word for word from the textbook. Packback is a breeze. Definitely a GPA booster if nothing else.
easy grader. nice. sick a lot so be prepared to read the textbook.
This class was extremely easy. The exams were open book and you had a whole week to do them once they were posted. Lectures said the same things that were in the textbook.

He was very disorganized throughout the entire semester. He always canceled lectures last minute, and when he didn't it was hard to stay engaged. However, the mid terms and final were open book and take home, so you could work in groups if you wanted. It was easy to get a good grade, but the lectures were unbearable if they happened at all.

I wish the kids in my class would appreciate what they have in Jordana. She is a genuine person who clearly puts her heart into each lecture, and gives us info we are both interested in, and that we can use in later classes.  Yeah, you have to put in the work.  But that just makes us better critical thinkers.  Highly recommend this brilliant woman!

She's an alright professor, its quite obvious its her first time teaching but she tries, lectures are mandatory but basically just a summarization of the chapter. She mainly reads out of the textbook and verbalizes the examples already provided.
woooo mamma
zzzzzzzzzz is right!!

The best prof I've had at SFU. He explains concepts really well, makes everything easy to understand, half of the midterm  questions were from assignments and MP, the final was harder than the midterms but still fair. He is very willing to help and meet outside office hours and is super funny. I wish he'd teach all physics courses!
No Comments

If she spends a lot of time on something, it doesn't mean that it is necessarily important. Correct terminology is very important for the lecture exams. Lab exam is hard but doable if you start prep on time (I didn't). No point in buying the textbook. Going to lectures doesn't help much, but she has in-class quizzes that are worth 5%.

Her labs were fun and her lectures were interactive which made it easier to follow along with topics. Definitely cares about het students and also got us all Tim bits one class. The midterm covers the first 1/2 of the course and the final covers the 1/2 half of the course, but the midterm was way easier than the final so don't be fooled.

Dr. Mladenovic was one of the first profs I had at SFU, and she was honestly really great. She is extremely caring and always did her very best to engage us in the material she was teaching, even buying the entire class of 200 donuts on one occasion. She is very sweet and the lectures/exams were fair, but you definitely need to study hard.

Dr. M is very accommodating. We had a TA strike so she made the midterm open book and MC. However, she is a very tough grader and expects us to use correct and precise key words otherwise we don't get marks for the question. This makes it harder to do well on short answer questions in her exams... I believe the course was scaled though
Would not recommend, the lab exam was brutal. Boring lectures as well, slides also horrible.

Dr. M is open minded and great at explaining the course concepts. Attending tutorials counts towards your grade and are recommended since some of the content on the exams may be from tutorial material. Dr M and her TAs are very explicit about what may or may not be on the tests. Very approachable and grades fairly. Make in depth notes on lectures!

i took BISC 101 and 102 with her. i don't really have any complaints. i found 101 a lot easier because 102 is more conceptual, but they are both doable. she seems to really care about how well of a job she is doing, and is really conscious of how students feel about the lectures. no complaints.
Horrible teacher and tough marker. GPA dropper for sure. Regret taking this class with her. Take it with someone else!

the class was manageable, all material on tests is on slides, so know the slides inside out. My strategy was to make flashcards on all the slides and that was how I studied. He is definitely passionate about the topics and can explain them well in my opinion. He adds in interesting current biology topics like CRISPR and 3 parent families too.
GUYS HE IS AWSOME BELIEVE ME IF U GET A CHANCE TAKE IT WITH HIM!! his goal is not to fail u just memorize his lectures and u will be fine

I LOVE TONY I DON'T UNDERstand why everyone is so stressed by him HONESTLY study the lectures and you will do just fine cuz all of his exams are from his lectures also he is so caring and always ready to answer questions in details and HE IS FUNNY. I dont know what yall want but he is a 1000/10 to me

Professor Williams is well-known as a first year gate keeper. Doing well in his class requires you to practically memorize his lecture. Nothing else is needed to get an A. 

Memorize the EXACT wording of his lectures, especially coloured key words. He WILL ask you and the EXACT copied wording from his slides will be required to get full marks.

He takes the time to put the essential information for students on his power points and presents them in an organized manner that will help students succeed. The course is fairly easy with the exception of the labs.
awesome prof
